14 May 2026 Military News

Operations

Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

  • Joint Task Force-Southern Border Marks One Year of Success, Bolstering Southern Border Security Through Integrated Operations US Northern Command 14 May 2026 -- Joint Task Force-Southern Border (JTF-SB) marks its one-year anniversary, March 14, 2026. This milestone highlights JTF-SB's first 365 days of executing agile, full-scale, and partner-focused missions in support of the Department of Homeland Security's (DHS) southern border mission.

  • Coast Guard Cutter Tahoma busts cocaine 'triple threat,' interdicting nearly $45.8 million worth of illicit narcotics off Colombia US Coast Guard 14 May 2026 -- Coast Guard Cutter Tahoma's crew simultaneously interdicted three suspected smuggling vessels carrying approximately 6,085 pounds of cocaine worth nearly $45.8 million, May 8, approximately 90 miles off Cartagena, Colombia. This seizure represents 2.3 million potentially lethal doses of cocaine that will not reach American streets.

    • Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Centcom Commander Says Epic Fury Crippled Iran, Enhanced Military Partnerships in Region DoD News 14 May 2026 -- The U.S. launched Operation Epic Fury in late February, and since then, American military forces in the U.S. Central Command area of operations have crippled Iran's military and its ability to project power, according to War Department leaders. The operation also highlighted military partners in the region.

  • Joint statement on the Multinational Military Mission for the Strait of Hormuz: 12 May 2026 UK MOD 14 May 2026 -- Joint statement from the United Kingdom, France, Albania, Australia, Bahrain, Belgium, Bulgaria, Canada, Croatia, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, Germany, Greece, Japan, Kosovo, Latvia, Lithuania, Montenegro, Netherlands, Portugal, Qatar, Republic of Korea, Slovakia, Sweden.

  • May 14: Iranian officials issue defiant messages, vow no surrender to whims of imperial powers Press TV 14 May 2026 -- Iranian officials issued a defiant message on Thursday, portraying the country's resilience and regional profile as stronger than ever before in the wake of the US-Israeli war of aggression.
  • Iranian Foreign Minister Says Hormuz Strait 'Open' If Ships 'Cooperate' With Tehran RFE/RL 14 May 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi said on May 14 that the Strait of Hormuz is open to all commercial vessels as long as they "cooperate" with Iran's naval forces.
  • Iran FM says US strangling Hormuz through illegal blockade, Strait open to cooperative vessels Press TV 14 May 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi tells Press TV that it is the United States, not Iran, that is strangling the Strait of Hormuz through its illegal blockade, amid heightened tensions in the critical maritime corridor through which a significant share of the world's energy trade passes.
  • Passage through the Strait of Hormuz by a Japan-related vessel Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 14 May 2026
  • Takaichi Says Second Japanese-Linked Ship Passes Through Strait Of Hormuz RFE/RL 14 May 2026 -- Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi announced the successful passage of one of the country's ships that had been stuck in the Persian Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz.

  • US Admiral Says Iran No Longer Poses Same Regional Threat RFE/RL 14 May 2026 -- Admiral Brad Cooper said that recent US strikes have dramatically weakened Iran's military capabilities, reducing its ability to threaten neighboring countries and American interests.
  • Rubio: It's In China's Interest To Pressure Iran To Open Strait Of Hormuz RFE/RL 14 May 2026 -- US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said it is in China's interest to pressure the Iranian government to open the Strait of Hormuz.
  • Rubio: US Wants China to Play More Active Role on Iran Sputnik 14 May 2026 -- US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said that during US President Donald Trump's visit to Beijing, the American delegation hopes to convince China's leadership to play a more active role in the context of Iran's plans in the Persian Gulf.
  • US House Votes Down Bid to Limit Trump's War Powers By Narrowest of Margins RFE/RL 14 May 2026 -- The US House of Representatives by the slimmest of margins defeated ⁠a ⁠Democratic-led resolution that sought to stop the war in Iran until hostilities are approved by Congress.

  • Iran parl. speaker warns US theatrics in Hormuz could trigger new global financial crisis Press TV 14 May 2026 -- Iran's Majlis (Parliament) speaker has warned that the United States' efforts at sustaining military escalation near the Strait of Hormuz could trigger a fresh global financial crisis at a time when Washington's national debt already stands at a whopping $39 trillion.
  • Test us again, you will repeat your failures: Iran FM to adversaries Press TV 14 May 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has warned the United States and the Israeli regime against attempting fresh military action against Iran, saying past confrontations had failed to achieve their objectives and any future attempt would yield the same outcome.
  • 'We will not give up the Strait of Hormuz at any cost': Iran's VP Aref Press TV 14 May 2026 -- Iran's Vice President Mohammad Reza Aref says the Strait of Hormuz belongs to Iran, stressing that "we will not give it up at any cost."
  • Iran to UAE: Hiding behind false claims is not possible, you are an aggressor Press TV 14 May 2026 -- A senior Iranian official has slammed the United Arab Emirates for being an aggressor, not merely a supporter of aggression, stating that the UAE cannot hide its aggressive nature and role behind false claims and fabricated narratives.
  • UAE 'active partner' in US-Israeli aggression on Iran: FM Araghchi Press TV 14 May 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has slammed the United Arab Emirates for being an active partner in the US-Israeli aggression against Iran, responding sharply to baseless claims made by a UAE representative.
  • US 'engaging in piracy' as Tehran defends tanker seizures: Iran Judiciary Press TV 14 May 2026 -- Iran's Judiciary spokesman Asghar Jahangiri has sharply criticized the US over Washington's "piracy activities," stressing that Iran's seizures of US-linked tankers in the Strait of Hormuz and across the Persian Gulf are carried out under clear domestic and international legal authority.
  • BRICS meeting: Araghchi says US empire in decline, Iran will never bow to pressure Press TV 14 May 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says the Iranian people have "firmly and proudly" stood up against horrific violence and will never surrender to the will and whims of the US imperial power, which is "in decline."
  • 'Absurd, deceptive': Tehran slams US for using 'coercion' to get support for anti-Iran Hormuz resolution Press TV 14 May 2026 -- The Iranian Permanent Mission to the United Nations has slammed the United States for using "coercion" to rally support for its politically-motivated draft resolution targeting Iran's legitimate actions in the Strait of Hormuz.

  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with Foreign Minister of the Islamic Republic of Iran Abbas Araghchi Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 14 May 2026
  • Russia vows to closely work with Iran, 'strongly condemns' US-Israeli war Press TV 14 May 2026 -- Russia has pledged to work closely with Iran to find peaceful solutions to the West Asia crisis within the framework of international law, while strongly condemning US and Israeli attacks on the Islamic Republic and praising the unity of the Iranian people in the face of external threats.

  • India Says Sinking Of Ship Near Oman 'Unacceptable' RFE/RL 14 May 2026 -- India's External Affairs Ministry called an attack on a ship flying its flag that it said took place on May 13 off the coast of Oman "unacceptable."
  • UAE Denies Netanyahu Visited During Iran War RFE/RL 14 May 2026 -- The United Arab Emirates denied on May 13 that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu visited the country during the war with Iran, rejecting claims by Netanyahu's office that he "secretly" met with UAE President Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • US procures 10,000 cheap cruise missiles as war on Iran strains stock: Report Press TV 14 May 2026 -- Facing rapidly shrinking missile stockpiles after heavy weapons use in the war against Iran, the Pentagon is rushing to procure more than 10,000 low-cost cruise missiles over the next three years, a report says.
  • Innovation, readiness converge in African Lion 26 counter-UAS advancement training Army News 14 May 2026 -- U.S. service members focused on advancing innovation to maintain battlefield superiority amid rapidly evolving threats and emerging technologies during African Lion 26.
  • Africom Commander Says Continent at 'Crossroads' of Global Commerce, Security DoD News 14 May 2026 -- Air Force Gen. Dagvin R.M. Anderson, commander of U.S. Africa Command, updated Congress on the security environment of the world's second largest continent during a hearing before the Senate Armed Services Committee in Washington today.
  • South Dakota Guard, Suriname Partners Conclude Operation Rumble in the Jungle SOUTHCOM 14 May 2026 -- South Dakota National Guard Soldiers and their Suriname partners have concluded Operation Rumble in the Jungle, a joint exercise that included testing the forces' ability to operate together in demanding jungle conditions.
  • U.S., Cameroon Boost Maritime Security with Unmanned Systems During Obangame Express 2026 US Navy 14 May 2026 -- U.S. Sixth Fleet successfully integrated advanced unmanned surface vessels (USVs) during Exercise Obangame Express 2026 in Douala, Cameroon.
  • Army, interagency, industry and local leaders unite at inaugural DCI Summit US Army 14 May 2026 -- The U.S. Army announced the secretary of the Army's inaugural Defense Critical Infrastructure Summit, uniting federal, state, local and private-sector leaders to forge a unified "whole-of-nation" approach to defending the infrastructure that powers Army readiness. The XVIII Airborne Corps hosted the summit at Fort Bragg, North Carolina.

  • Airpower underpins Swedish-led exercise AURORA 26 NATO Allied Air Command 14 May 2026 -- Swedish and Allied Air Forces rehearsed distributed Airpower during AURORA 26, Sweden's first Aurora exercise as a NATO Ally.
  • Open Spirit 2026 delivers real world impact in the Baltic Sea NATO Allied Maritime Command 14 May 2026 -- NATO ships took part in an international mine countermeasures exercise off the coast of Latvia between 1 and 13 May 2026. Open Spirit 26 brought together Allied and Partner navies, enhanced regional security, strengthened interoperability, and reduced the threat posed by historical naval mines in the region.

  • Poland's predator in Ukrainian service: an overview of the Rosomak APC Ukraine MOD 14 May 2026 -- Polish-made Rosomak armoured personnel carriers have been in service with the Defence Forces of Ukraine for several years. Service members highlight the vehicle's high manoeuvrability and the firepower of its weapons systems.

  • Cabinet weighing options to fund weapons excluded from budget bill CNA 14 May 2026 -- Premier Cho Jung-tai (卓榮泰) said Thursday that the Cabinet is considering ways to fund key weapons programs excluded from a supplementary budget bill passed by the Legislature last week.

  • New generation of ADF space personnel graduates from first space operations course Australia DOD 14 May 2026 -- A new generation of Australian Defence Force (ADF) space personnel has graduated from Defence's first space operations Initial Employment Training (IET) course.
  • Albanese Government signs Defence MOU with Norway, helping make Australia more self reliant and boosting regional security Australia DOD 14 May 2026 -- The Albanese Government has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the Norwegian government, to further support the acquisition and domestic manufacturing of missiles in Australia.

  • NZ Army platoon trains with Republic of Korea Marine Corps in historic first New Zealand Defence Force 14 May 2026 -- Soldiers from the New Zealand Army have recently completed training alongside the Republic of Korea Marine Corps (ROK MC) during a large-scale joint exercise near Pohang, North Gyeongsang Province.

  • Minister McGuinty concludes successful visit to United Arab Emirates and Qatar Canada 14 May 2026 -- Today, the Minister of National Defence, the Honourable David J. McGuinty, concluded a productive visit to the Middle East as part of the Government of Canada's broader effort to diversify defence partnerships and expand defence industrial cooperation.
  • Minister Sidhu meets with Philippines' Secretary of National Defence Canada 14 May 2026 -- Yesterday, the Honourable Maninder Sidhu, Minister of International Trade, met with Gilberto E. Teodoro Jr., Secretary of National Defense of the Philippines, in Manila.

  • Self-reliance and jointness must for strategic autonomy & future-ready force: Raksha Mantri India PIB 14 May 2026 -- "A nation's strength will increasingly depend on how quickly its defence forces, laboratories, and industries think & act as one," said Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh as he underscored the importance of self-reliance and jointness to secure strategic autonomy & remain future-ready for emerging security challenges. He was addressing policy makers, military leadership, defence industry stakeholders, diplomats, innovators, start-ups, academia & strategic experts through a video message during Kalam & Kavach 3.0, a defence strategic dialogue organised at Manekshaw Centre, New Delhi on May 14, 2026.

  • The 2nd Japan-EU Security and Defence Dialogue Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 14 May 2026
  • "Satellite constellation" begins operations to ensure effectiveness of long-range missiles: Japanese official People's Daily 14 May 2026 -- Japan's Parliamentary Vice-Minister of Defense Youhei Wakabayashi said Thursday that the country's information-gathering satellite system "satellite constellation" has been operational since April, in order to ensure the effectiveness of its long-range missiles used as part of Japan's "counterstrike capabilities," Kyodo News reported.

  • Defence delivering on diplomacy strategy as Minister visits Western Balkans and Türkiye UK MOD 14 May 2026 -- UK Defence Minister Lord Coaker visited Montenegro, Serbia, and Kosovo strengthening NATO partnerships and Euro-Atlantic security across the Western Balkans

  • United States Completes Accelerated Mission to Remove Highly Enriched Uranium from Venezuela US Dept. of State 14 May 2026 -- The United States, in partnership with Venezuela and the United Kingdom and with technical support from the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), successfully removed excess highly enriched uranium (HEU) from Venezuela's shuttered RV-1 research reactor.
  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio With Sean Hannity of Fox News Channel US Dept. of State 14 May 2026
  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio With Tom Llamas of NBC News US Dept. of State 14 May 2026

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 14 May 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 14 May 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • Defamation Lawsuit to be Filed against The New York Times Israel - Prime Minister 14 May 2026

  • Israeli strikes kill nearly a dozen civilians in Southern Lebanon, including women and children Press TV 14 May 2026 -- At least ten people, including women and children, have been killed, and several others wounded, as the Israeli military pressed ahead with air and artillery strikes against targets in southern Lebanon, despite a ceasefire agreement officially in effect.

  • Gaza reconstruction envisages building 'islands' out of rubble UN News 14 May 2026 -- A feasibility study is underway to examine whether Gaza's war debris could be recycled to reclaim coastal land and build artificial islands, as part of reconstruction.

  • OIC Assistant Secretary-General for Palestine and Al-Quds Affairs Consults with the Palestinian Ministry of Foreign Affairs on the Latest Developments in the Palestinian Cause Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 14 May 2026 -- Ambassador Dr. Dawas Dawas, Assistant Secretary-General for Palestine and Al-Quds Affairs at the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC), held several consultations at the Palestinian Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Expatriates. He began with a meeting with Her Excellency Dr. Varsen Aghabekian Shahin, Minister of Foreign Affairs and Expatriates, who expressed her appreciation for the OIC's role and its resolutions and positions in support of the Palestinian people and their legitimate rights, foremost among them their right to self-determination and the establishment of their independent state with Al-Quds (Jerusalem) as its capital.
  • No: 88, 14 May 2026, Regarding the Storming of Al-Aqsa Mosque by an Israeli Minister Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 14 May 2026
  • Netanyahu 'monster, disgrace to humanity': Pakistani minister Press TV 14 May 2026 -- Pakistan's Defense Minister Khawaja Muhammad Asif has described Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu as a "monster" and a disgrace to humanity for patronizing and promoting rape of Palestinian detainees.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Ukraine, US funding, Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Haiti UNOCHA 14 May 2026 -- UN vehicle hit by drone strikes while delivering aid

  • US Pledges Fresh $1.8 Billion For UN Aid Operations, Including Ukraine RFE/RL 14 May 2026 -- The United States has announced an additional $1.8 billion in humanitarian assistance for UN-led relief operations worldwide, including continued support for Ukraine.
  • United States Pledges Additional $1.8 Billion in Life-Saving Humanitarian Funding to OCHA's Humanitarian Reset & Shares Powerful Results from the Trump Administration's UN Humanitarian Reform Agreement US Dept. of State 14 May 2026 -- ccToday, building on the tremendous success of the Trump Administration's landmark December 2025 "Humanitarian Reset" framework agreement between the United States and the United Nations Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA), the United States announced an additional $1.8 billion in humanitarian funding for OCHA's country-based pooled funds and hyper-prioritized life-saving humanitarian assistance activities.
  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on the additional humanitarian contribution by the United States UN Secretary-General 14 May 2026
  • UN welcomes $1.8 billion US boost for humanitarian operations UN News 14 May 2026 -- An additional $1.8 billion in US humanitarian funding will allow the United Nations and its partners to expand emergency relief operations reaching millions of people worldwide, as rising global needs and funding shortfalls force aid agencies to scale back assistance.
  • Statement on US funding by Tom Fletcher, Under-Secretary-General for Humanitarian Affairs and Emergency Relief Coordinator, 14 May 2026 UNOCHA 14 May 2026
  • UN Relief Chief: US funding helped reach 14.4 million people already this year UNOCHA 14 May 2026 -- Remarks to the press on US humanitarian funding by Tom Fletcher, Under-Secretary-General for Humanitarian Affairs and Emergency Relief Coordinator

  • Yemen parties agree under UN mediation to release 1,600 detainees UN News 14 May 2026 -- More than 1,600 conflict-related detainees in Yemen will be released under a UN-brokered agreement reached after months of negotiations in Jordan, marking the largest prisoner release deal since the country's civil war began and offering a rare sign of progress in stalled peace efforts.
  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on the deal reached for the exchange of conflict-related detainees in Yemen UN Secretary-General 14 May 2026
  • Joint Forces Command: Seven Saudi POWs Among 1,750 to be Released in Agreement between All Yemeni Parties SPA 14 May 2026 -- Official Spokesperson of the Coalition to Restore Legitimacy in Yemen Major General Turki Al-Malki issued a statement today announcing that "an agreement has been signed today for the future release of 1,750 prisoners of war (POWs) and detainees from all Yemeni parties and coalition forces. The agreement included 27 from coalition forces, seven of whom are Saudi POWs

  • Drones Attack Kurdish Militia Camp In Iraq's Kurdistan RFE/RL 14 May 2026 -- Two drone attacks were reported on a camp of Kurdish militias that oppose Iran north of the city of Erbil, located in the Iraqi Kurdistan region.

  • Millions trapped in deepening hunger crisis in the DRC as needs far outpace humanitarian response FAO 14 May 2026 -- The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) and the United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) warn that the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) continues to face one of the world's largest and most severe hunger crises.

News Reports

  • FBI Washington Field Office Announces $200,000 Reward for Information Leading to Apprehension of Former U.S. Counterintelligence Agent Charged with Espionage for Iran FBI 14 May 2026 -- The FBI Washington Field Office today announced that the FBI is offering a $200,000 reward for information leading to the apprehension and prosecution of Monica Witt, a former U.S. service member and counterintelligence agent who was indicted by a federal grand jury in the District of Columbia in February 2019 on charges of espionage, including transmitting national defense information to the government of Iran.

  • National Intelligence Enterprise Subcommittee Holds Joint Law Enforcement Intel Sharing Roundtable with House Homeland Security Committee US House of Representatives Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence 14 May 2026 -- Today, the House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence Subcommittee on the National Intelligence Enterprise (NIE) held a joint open roundtable with the House Homeland Security Committee's Subcommittee on Counterterrorism and Intelligence to examine the working partnerships between federal, state, local, and tribal law enforcement and intelligence sharing required to support a robust homeland security posture. NIE Chairman Austin Scott (R-GA) co-led the roundtable discussion.

  • Follow-up message by the WHO Director-General to the people of Tenerife regarding the hantavirus response WHO 14 May 2026 -- Dear people of Tenerife,

  • High-Ranking CJNG Leader Charged with Federal Drug Trafficking Conspiracy, Firearm Offenses, and Money Laundering Conspiracy US Dept. of Justice 14 May 2026 -- A federal grand jury in the District of Columbia returned a superseding indictment yesterday against Audias Flores Silva, also known as "Jardinero," 45, of Mexico, a high-ranking leader of the Mexico-based drug trafficking organization the Cartel de Jalisco Nueva Generacion (CJNG).

  • The 8th Japan-Republic of Korea Counter-Terrorism Consultation Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 14 May 2026

  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's talks with Foreign Minister of India Subrahmanyam Jaishankar Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 14 May 2026
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with Minister of Foreign Affairs, Emigration, and Egyptian Expatriates of the Arab Republic of Egypt Badr Abdelatty Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 14 May 2026
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Federative Republic of Brazil Mauro Vieira Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 14 May 2026
  • West Freezes About $590bln Belonging to Russia, Cuba, Venezuela, Iran, North Korea - Shoigu Sputnik 14 May 2026 -- Western countries, according to the most conservative estimates, have frozen about $590 billion belonging to Russia, Cuba, Venezuela, Iraq, Iran, North Korea, Libya and Afghanistan, Russian Security Council Secretary Sergei Shoigu said on Thursday.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on May 14, 2026 PRC MofA 14 May 2026
  • Day One Of Trump-Xi Talks Covers Iran, Trade, And Taiwan RFE/RL 14 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping wrapped up more than two hours of talks in Beijing on May 14, with both sides agreeing that the Strait of Hormuz must remain open amid mounting global economic anxiety tied to Washington's standoff with Iran, according to the White House.
  • President Xi Jinping Holds Talks with U.S. President Donald J. Trump PRC MofA 14 May 2026 -- On the morning of May 14, President Xi Jinping held talks with U.S. President Donald J. Trump, who is on a state visit to China, at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing.
  • Chinese FM spokesperson elaborates on new positioning in China-US relations Global Times 14 May 2026 -- China stands ready to work with the US to translate the new vision into actions in the same direction and jointly work for a steady, sound and sustainable development of China-US relations, Guo Jiakun, spokesperson of Chinese Foreign Ministry, said on Thursday.
  • Xi holds welcome banquet for Trump PRC MND 14 May 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping on Thursday held a banquet at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing to welcome U.S. President Donald Trump on his state visit to China.
  • President Xi Jinping Holds Welcoming Banquet for U.S. President Donald J. Trump PRC MofA 14 May 2026 -- On the evening of May 14, President Xi Jinping held a welcoming banquet for U.S. President Donald J. Trump at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing.
  • Trump, Xi Open Beijing Summit Seeking Trade Stability Amid Wider US-China Rivalry RFE/RL 14 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping opened high-stakes talks in Beijing on May 14 after a lavish welcome ceremony, with both men emphasizing their personal relationship and the need for closer cooperation despite growing tensions between the world's two largest economies.
  • President Xi Jinping Holds Talks with U.S. President Donald J. Trump PRC MofA 14 May 2026 -- On the morning of May 14, President Xi Jinping held talks with U.S. President Donald J. Trump, who is on a state visit to China, at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing.
  • Discarded hard drives could become 'hidden bombs' for data leaks, strict disposal rules required for classified drives: MSS Global Times 14 May 2026 -- Discarded hard drives could become "hidden bombs" for data leaks, China's Ministry of State Security (MSS) warned on Thursday, after disclosing a case involving the resale of "new" drives that still carried improperly erased internal data. The agency also stressed that idle hard drives containing classified information must never be discarded or resold randomly, according to a notice published on the ministry's official WeChat account.
  • Chinese private rocket firm completes high-capacity launch mission Global Times 14 May 2026 -- The Zhuque-2E Y5 carrier rocket, developed by Chinese commercial aerospace firm LandSpace, blasted off from the company's launch site at Dongfeng Commercial Space Innovation Test Zone on Thursday. The rocket successfully completed all flight mission procedures as planned, and the launch was declared a complete success, according to the company.

  • U.S. Taiwan policy unchanged after Trump-Xi summit in Beijing: Rubio CNA 14 May 2026 -- U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Thursday in Beijing that U.S. policy on Taiwan has not changed, following the summit between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping (習近平).
  • Xi's Taiwan remarks at Trump-Xi summit seen as warning to U.S.: Experts CNA 14 May 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping's (習近平) Taiwan-related remarks during his meeting with U.S. President Donald Trump in Beijing Thursday were seen by Taiwanese experts as a warning that Washington's handling of the Taiwan issue could risk major conflict with China.
  • U.S., EU support Taiwan after no WHA invite for 10th straight year CNA 14 May 2026 -- The United States and the European Union both expressed support for Taiwan's participation in international organizations after Taipei was not invited to the upcoming World Health Assembly (WHA) for the 10th straight year.
  • Opposition slams Lai ahead of May 19 impeachment vote CNA 14 May 2026 -- Opposition legislators on Thursday accused President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) of refusing to accept democratic checks and balances after he did not attend legislative review sessions on an impeachment motion against him ahead of a final vote scheduled for May 19.
  • MOFA says China 'sole risk to peace' after Xi's Taiwan remarks to Trump CNA 14 May 2026 -- Taiwan's Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MOFA) on Thursday said Beijing was "the sole risk to regional peace and stability," after Chinese President Xi Jinping (習近平) told U.S. President Donald Trump that Taiwan was "the most important issue" in China-U.S. relations.
  • Xi warns Trump: US meddling in Chinese Taipei risks major conflict Press TV 14 May 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping has cautioned visiting US President Donald Trump against Washington's "mishandling" of the issue of Chinese Taipei, warning that improper US actions could trigger serious tensions in bilateral relations.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 14 May 2026

  • Iran Has Carried Out Nearly 30 Political Executions Since Start Of US-Israel War RFE/RL 14 May 2026 -- Iran executed two men on May 13 as the accelerated campaign of political hangings since the start of the war with the United States and Israel shows no signs of abating.

  • Iran reaffirms brotherly ties a top priority as Iraq forms new government Press TV 14 May 2026 -- Iran has reaffirmed that expanding brotherly relations with Iraq remains at the top of its foreign policy priorities following the formation of a new government in Baghdad.
  • No: 90, 14 May 2026, Regarding the Formation of the New Government in Iraq Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 14 May 2026

  • 'We have absolutely no fuel': Cuba energy minister warns of collapse amid 'genocidal' US blockade Press TV 14 May 2026 -- Cuba's energy and mines minister has warned that the country has run out of diesel and fuel oil, saying US sanctions and restrictions have left the island's power system in crisis.

  • Transcript of the Press Briefing by the Spokesperson on Thursday, 14th May 2026 Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 14 May 2026

  • Press conference - Canberra Prime Minister of Australia 14 May 2026

  • Prime Minister Carney announces forthcoming National Electricity Strategy Prime Minister of Canada 14 May 2026 -- The world is changing rapidly. The United States is upending global trade, wars are ongoing in Europe and the Middle East, artificial intelligence is scaling at speed, and climate change is intensifying. In response, Canada's new government is focused on what we can control: building an affordable, competitive, and sustainable Canadian economy. Central to this mission is supplying and controlling our own energy.
  • Prime Minister Carney announces forthcoming National Electricity Strategy Prime Minister of Canada 14 May 2026

  • "We hope to deliver larger quantities of oil from Kazakhstan to global markets through our country" Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 14 May 2026 -- Speaking at the closing session of the Türkiye-Kazakhstan Business Forum, President Erdoğan said: "Türkiye is a country which has ensured its energy supply security by diversifying its resources years earlier. We hope to deliver larger quantities of oil from Kazakhstan to global markets through our country."
  • "The significance of the Trans-Caspian East-West Middle Corridor is better understood each passing day" Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 14 May 2026 -- Speaking at a joint press conference with President Tokayev of Kazakhstan, President Erdoğan said: "The significance of the Trans-Caspian East-West Middle Corridor, today's equivalent of the Silk Road, is better understood each passing day. We, together with Kazakhstan and our other partners, will continue to promote this corridor for the transportation of energy resources to the West, not just for goods shipment."

  • World News in Brief: Malnutrition in Somalia, hunger crisis in DR Congo, energy shortages in Cuba UN News 14 May 2026 -- Nearly six million people in Somalia - almost a third of the population analysed - are projected to face acute hunger between April and June, with 1.9 million expected to experience emergency levels, UN-backed food security experts warned on Thursday.
